headline: 3 Equal Weight ETFs Beating the S&P 500 in 2026 as Mega-Cap Dominance Fades
summary: The S&P 500 you own through a standard index fund is not as diversified as it looks. The cap-weighted version of the index hands roughly a third of your money to a handful of mega-cap technology names, meaning five or six companies effectively drive your returns. Equal-weight ETFs fix that by giving every holding an ... 3 Equal Weight ETFs Beating the S&P 500 in 2026 as Mega-Cap Dominance Fades

headline: SCHD Just Made Big Changes. Is This Dividend Growth ETF Still a Buy?
summary: The Schwab U.S. Dividend Equity ETF (NYSEARCA:SCHD) stands as one of the most popular dividend-growth vehicles on the market. Since its inception on October 20, 2011, the fund has delivered a cumulative total return of 478%, equating to a 13.3% annualized average. With a current yield of 3.3%, SCHD has turned a hypothetical $10,000 investment ... SCHD Just Made Big Changes. Is This Dividend Growth ETF Still a Buy?

headline: Faith-based investor coalition sues UnitedHealth to force disclosure of M&A impacts
summary: The shareholders, members of the Interfaith Center on Corporate Responsibility, have been trying to get the healthcare juggernaut to share more information about its controversial business practices for more than a year.

headline: UnitedHealth Faces Sole Antitrust Spotlight As Valuation Gap Widens
summary: CVS Health has reached a proposed settlement with the Federal Trade Commission in a major antitrust case focused on pharmacy benefit managers. The settlement leaves UnitedHealth Group (NYSE:UNH) as the sole remaining defendant in the U.S. government’s ongoing lawsuit. This shift increases regulatory focus on UnitedHealth Group’s pharmacy benefit manager operations and its role in the broader case. UnitedHealth Group, trading at $272.28, now faces concentrated attention in a high profile...
